confirm.title: Set up {name}
confirm.description:
Home Assistant detected your xBloom machine over Bluetooth. Confirm to add it.

No account, no MQTT broker, no setup beyond this — recipes are added afterwards via the integration's *Configure* menu (paste a share URL from the iOS app).

user.title: Set up xBloom Studio
user.description: Pick your machine from the list (Home Assistant's Bluetooth integration sees {discovered_count} right now). If your machine isn't listed, type its BLE name — it's printed in the iOS app under *Settings → Machine* (something like *XBLOOM ABC123*).

reauth_confirm.title: Log in to xBloom again
reauth_confirm.description: Your xBloom cloud session for {email} has expired and can't be refreshed. Enter your password to log in again and resume recipe sync.

error.ble_name_required: Pick a machine from the list, or type its BLE name.
error.ble_name_invalid: BLE names look like 'XBLOOM ABC123'. Check the value from the iOS app's machine settings.
error.cloud_login_failed: Login failed. Check your xBloom email and password and try again.

abort.already_configured: This xBloom machine is already set up.
abort.not_supported: This Bluetooth device doesn't look like an xBloom machine.
abort.reauth_successful: Logged in again — recipe sync resumed.

init.title: xBloom recipe library
init.description: Create, edit, or remove recipes. Log in to your xBloom account to sync recipes with the cloud (optional) — otherwise recipes are stored locally.

menu_options.add_recipe: Add a recipe by share URL

firmware_flashing.title: Enable firmware flashing
firmware_flashing.description: Off by default. When on, the Firmware entity gets an Install button that flashes updates to the machine over Bluetooth. The transfer is validated byte-for-byte against a real xBloom update, but ⚠️ flashing firmware can brick the machine if the Bluetooth link drops mid-update, and this has not yet been tested on live hardware. Only enable it if you accept that risk.

cloud_login.title: Log in to xBloom cloud
cloud_login.description: Sign in with your xBloom account to sync recipes and unlock cloud features (create/edit recipes from the cloud, firmware updates). Turn on *Remember my credentials* to have the session refresh itself automatically — your password is then stored locally in Home Assistant (only ever sent to xBloom's login endpoint). Leave it off for more privacy: you'll be asked to log in again whenever the session expires.

cloud_reconcile.title: You have local recipes
cloud_reconcile.description: Once you log in, the xBloom cloud becomes the single source of truth for your recipes. What should happen to the {count} recipe(s) currently stored locally?

menu_options.cloud_upload: Upload my local recipes to the cloud
menu_options.cloud_replace: Delete local recipes and use the cloud

cloud_logout.title: Log out of xBloom cloud
cloud_logout.description: Log out of {email}? Recipes will revert to the local library (your synced recipes stay cached locally). Firmware updates and cloud recipe editing will be unavailable until you log in again.

add_recipe.title: Add a recipe
add_recipe.description: Open the recipe in the xBloom iOS app, tap Share → Copy Link, and paste it here. Or paste just the share id (the value after `?id=`).

create_recipe.title: New recipe — pick a cup
create_recipe.description: Name your recipe and pick the cup type. The next step's dose range is locked to what the chosen cup supports (xPod = 15 g; Omni dripper = 5–18 g; Other = 5–25 g; Tea = 1–5 g).

create_recipe_pours.title: Tweak the pours
create_recipe_pours.description: Target: {total_ml} ml ({dose} g × {ratio}). Pre-filled at {per_pour_ml} ml each. Current total: {current_total_ml} ml. All pours must sum to exactly {total_ml} ml — too little or too much will be rejected.

edit_recipe.title: Edit a recipe
edit_recipe.description: Pick the recipe to edit. The next step lets you change the brewing parameters.

write_to_slot.title: Write to machine slot?
write_to_slot.description: Optional: write {recipe_name} to a machine slot (A/B/C) so it's brewable from the machine alone. Pick 'skip' to finish.

create_recipe_brew.title: Brew parameters
create_recipe_brew.description: Cup type: {cup}. Dose stepper is locked to the cup's allowed range. Ratio is the brew ratio 1:N (5–25, step 0.5).

error.invalid_share: Couldn't fetch a recipe from that URL/id. Double-check the link from the xBloom app.
error.ratio_invalid: Ratio must be between 1:5 and 1:25 in 0.5 steps (e.g. 1:5, 1:15.5, 1:25).
error.grind_out_of_range: Grind size must be between 1 and 80.
error.rpm_out_of_range: Grinder speed must be between 60 and 120 RPM.
error.pour_count_mismatch: Pour count must be between 1 and 9 and match the number of pours.
error.cup_type_invalid: Pick Omni dripper, xPod, or Other.
error.volume_total_mismatch: Pour volumes must sum to dose × ratio (within 1 ml).
error.temp_out_of_range: Pour temperature must be between 40 and 98 °C.
error.flow_out_of_range: Flow rate must be between 3.0 and 3.5 ml/s.
error.pause_out_of_range: Pause must be between 0 and 59 seconds.
error.pattern_invalid: Pattern must be centered, circular, or spiral.
error.bypass_volume_required: Bypass water volume is required when bypass is enabled.
error.bypass_temp_required: Bypass water temperature is required when bypass is enabled.
error.slot_write_failed: Couldn't write to machine slot. Make sure the machine is powered on and try again, or pick skip.
error.dose_out_of_range_for_cup: Dose is outside the dripper's allowed range. Omni dripper accepts 5–18 g; Other accepts 5–25 g; xPod is locked at 15 g; Tea accepts 1–5 g.
error.volume_out_of_range: Each pour volume must be between 0 and 240 ml.
error.credentials_required: Enter both your email and password.
error.cloud_login_failed: Login failed. Check your xBloom email and password and try again.
